strings.s: Running in the background. Double-click the icon to open.

Welcome to VirusTotal Scanner!

• Scan files/folders by drag-and-drop or the right-click menu.
• Add a VirusTotal API key to get started.
Would you like to add a VirusTotal API key now?
Add 'Scan with VirusTotal' to the right-click menu?\n(For all users; administrator permission / UAC will be requested.)
The right-click menu entry points to an old exe path (the app was moved). Repair it now?
You can also drag and drop files/folders here.
The language change takes full effect after the app is restarted.
You can add multiple keys; when one is exhausted it switches to the next.
Don't show in the menu for safe types (txt, image, video…)
Files with a valid code signature aren't sent to VT to save quota.\nNote: signature trust = the publisher is verified, not a guarantee of "clean".
Don't send signed files to VT (keyless, unlimited)
Keyless lookup: open VirusTotal via the GUI (slow, no quota)
The chosen folder is scanned periodically (keyless); the result is written to an HTML report.
Minimize to the system tray on close
Show a notification when a threat is found
Watch Downloads — auto-scan new files (Downloads + Desktop)
Downloads watching {0}. Takes full effect after restarting the app.
Start with Windows (in the background, in the tray)
Ask about an interrupted scan at startup
Resume an interrupted scan at startup WITHOUT asking
Confirmations you marked 'don't ask again' show up here; you can re-enable asking.

{0} v{1}

Usage:
 VirusTotalScanner.exe [options] <file|folder> [<file|folder> ...]

Double-click to open the GUI. Run from a terminal to use command-line mode.

Options:
 -s, --scan Scan flag (used by the right-click menu)
 -r, --recurse Scan folders including subfolders
 --no-trust Ignore signature trust (send signed files to VT too)
 -k, --keyless Keyless lookup (via GUI/WebView2, no quota, slow)
 --expand-archives Expand archives (zip/nupkg/jar…), scan members separately
 --running Scan all running process images ("am I infected right now?")
 -n, --nogui, --cli Run in the terminal without opening the GUI
 -g, --gui Open the GUI even from a terminal
 -j, --json Write results as JSON (stdout)
 --report <path> Write a report file (.html/.csv/.json/.txt — format from extension)
 --fail-on <N> Exit code 1 on any file with N+ detections (CI gate)
 --diff <json> Compare against a prior --report json (sha256); write delta
 --fail-on-new / --fail-on-regression exit 1 on a new/regressed verdict
 -q, --quiet Plain output (verdict lines only)
 --install Add to the right-click menu
 --uninstall Remove from the right-click menu
 --repair Repair the right-click menu entry (exe path)
 --addkey <KEY> Add an API key (stored encrypted)
 --listkeys List configured keys and quotas
 --removekey <id|all> Remove key(s)
 --lookup <hash> Look up an MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256 hash
 --expect <hash> Verify a file against an expected hash (exit code 4 on mismatch)
 -h, --help This help
 -v, --version Version

Exit codes: 0 clean, 1 threat found, 2 usage/IO error, 3 no key, 4 hash mismatch.

Note: This is a GUI app; use 'Start-Process -Wait' to wait in a script.
Running processes: {0} images to scan ({1} unreadable/skipped).
ERROR: no API key, signature-skip is off and keyless (GUI) mode is not enabled.
(Warning: no key — only signed files can be evaluated, unsigned ones are skipped.)
(Keyless GUI mode on — lookups go through WebView2, no quota but slow.)
Diff: baseline could not be read:
ERROR: --expect wants a single file path.
ERROR: the expected hash must be 32 (MD5), 40 (SHA-1) or 64 (SHA-256) hex characters.

VirusTotalScanner will add itself to the Windows right-click menu (all users).
Administrator permission (UAC) will be requested.

Continue?
Added to the right-click menu.\nOn Windows 11 it appears under 'Show more options'.
The operation could not be completed (administrator permission may be required).
Got a response but couldn't read the quota (the key may still work).
Total {0} • Done {1} • Malicious {2} • Suspicious {3} • Clean {4} • Signed↷skipped {5} • Errors {6}
Add an API key before scanning, or enable signature-skip / keyless (GUI) mode in Trust Sources.

The selection contains an archive (zip/nupkg/jar…).

Expand its members and look each one up separately (no quota)?

Yes = scan members • No = scan the archive itself
MD5 / SHA-1 / SHA-256 hash to look up:
This hash was not found on VirusTotal.
Clear the local hash cache ({0} entries)?
Keyless (GUI) mode enabled. Upcoming files will be looked up without quota.
No files to re-check (no cache entry older than {0} days).
{0} cache entries (older than {1} days) will be re-checked.\nKeyless (via GUI) — may take a while. Continue?
No autostart hook found for '{0}' (Run/Startup/Tasks are clean).
Note: these are only listed; removal must be done manually (regedit / Task Scheduler).
No watched files.\nRight-click a result and pick 'Add to integrity watch'.
No readable running process image found.

{0} currently running process images will be scanned ({1} unreadable/skipped).
Most are Microsoft-signed so they're skipped; only unknown ones go to VT.

Continue?
This file's folder was not found.
File to verify against the expected hash
The expected hash must be 32 (MD5), 40 (SHA-1) or 64 (SHA-256) hex characters.
The file needs a VT result first.
Quarantine '{0}'? (renamed to .VIRUS, made non-executable; can be restored later)
File quarantined (non-executable). It can be restored from the quarantine vault.
Search the disk for other byte-identical (SHA-256) copies of '{0}' (no quota). Continue?
WARNING: '{0}' still looks malicious ({1}/{2}). Restore anyway?

All API keys are out of quota.
Soonest reset: {0:HH:mm} (local time).

What would you like to do?
🌐 Switch to keyless (GUI) mode — no quota, slower
{0} folders • {1} files • {2} threats • {3} suspicious • {4} clean • {5} signed-skipped • {6} unknown/error
No 2+ distinct hashes share a family (no repeated family in the cache).
{0} family cluster(s) — distinct files sharing the same malware family.
trust chain could not be built
🕗 {0}/{1} detections come from months-old signatures — worth re-checking
🤖 All detections are heuristic/ML (no signature match → likely false positive)
Be careful — don't run it yet
Not found on VirusTotal (unknown){0}. Unknown files carry higher risk.
only minor/low-reputation engines flagged it (likely a false positive)
all detections are heuristic/ML (no signature match)
the community mostly flagged it harmless ({0}/{1} votes)
0 detections but a very new/rare file{0}.
🔗 Connected threats: {0} files share a common IOC
Select a file on the left to see details.
📎 {0} appended after the signature — could be an installer, but could also be stuffed/trojanized

A valid code signature was found; not sent to VirusTotal to save quota.
Note: signature trust = the publisher is verified, not a guarantee of "clean".
To send it to VT anyway, right-click the row in the queue and pick "Ignore trust, scan with VT".
• (cache: engine list not stored, rescan for detail)
Scan a folder, including its subfolders.
Look up an MD5/SHA hash on VirusTotal (no file needed).
Verify a file matches the expected hash exactly.
Scan the images of all running processes — 'am I infected right now?'.
Check whether the files you are monitoring have changed (drift).
Save the results as a CSV table.
Write the results to an HTML/CSV/JSON/text report.
Summarize scanned folders with threat/clean counts.
Group different files that share the same malware family.
View quarantined items; restore them if safe.
Clear the local hash cache (verdicts will be fetched from VT again).
Group executables arriving on disk by arrival day.
Reach every feature from one place (find copies, autostart hooks, family clusters…).
⚠ The last file you scanned at this path was DIFFERENT ({0:yyyy-MM-dd} — {1} {2}); the content has CHANGED since then.
🕘 You have scanned this file {0} time(s) before. Previous: {1:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm} — {2} {3}
The clipboard is empty or contains no text.
The clipboard text is not a file path, folder, or hash:\n{0}
{0} has been quarantined, but {1} autostart hook(s) still point to it (Windows will try to run it every boot). Clean them up now?
This file is running right now: {0}.\nClose it first to quarantine?
{0} is still locked; it has been marked for deletion on the next reboot. Please restart.
This file has no hash yet; it cannot be marked clean.
This folder is already in the list.
{0} files marked as clean (will be skipped in scans from now on).
Quarantine {0} files? (their extensions become .VIRUS, cannot run; can be restored later)
Malicious {0} · Suspicious {1} · Clean {2} · Signed {3} · Skipped {4} · Error {5} ({6}/{7})
Pick a way to start scanning
You can also drag and drop files/folders here
Scan a folder with its subfolders
Look up an MD5/SHA hash on VirusTotal
Scan the path/hash on the clipboard
Check the file path, folder, or MD5/SHA hash on the clipboard
Compare a file against the expected hash
Scan the images of all running processes
Look for change/drift in monitored files
Group files that share the same malware family
Group incoming executables by arrival time
Summarize scanned folders with threat counts
Write results to an HTML/CSV/JSON/text report
Search the disk for exact copies of the selected file
Search for persistence entries for the selected file
Other files in the selected file's folder
Save the current scan settings as a named profile
The Downloads folder was not found.
All files in the allowlist are still clean.
{0} files are now flagged! Review the red rows (remove them or count as clean).
{0} clean files added to the allowlist from history.
Files you said 'Mark as clean' for live here; 'Health check' re-looks them up keylessly and flags in red any that later turn into a threat.
Files under these folders are skipped during scanning — for build output whose hash changes on every build (a hash list can't cover it).
The first matching rule is applied. Actions: ToastOnly (notify only), MarkClean (add to allowlist), SuppressFolder (skip the folder), Quarantine (quarantine). Empty list = built-in behavior.
Show a summary notification when the scan finishes
Suggest a scan when a USB drive is plugged in
Auto-scan immediately without clicking when a USB drive is plugged in (in the background)
Check every launched exe as it starts (real-time, requires administrator)
This protection takes effect on the next launch (WMI monitoring requires administrator rights).
Mark 1-2 heuristic detections on a signed file as 'softened by signature' (likely false positive)
Let background watchers auto-quarantine high-detection threats (reversible)
Mute notifications while a full-screen app (game/presentation) is active
Auto-clean quarantine entries older than this many days (0=off):
In the tray, re-check in the background every N hours (watch/cache/integrity, 0=on startup only):
Reset all settings to their default values? (API keys are not affected)
Settings reset to default. Reopen the Settings tab for the change to show.
Settings exported (API keys excluded, safe to share).
{0} settings imported. Reopen the Settings tab for the change to show.
shares a network indicator with {0} scanned files (may be the same campaign)
🔓 Likely false positive due to a valid signature: {0} — confirm with ‘Mark as clean’
👍 The community mostly voted harmless ({0}/{1} votes) — likely false positive
🧪 What this file does to your PC if it runs:
🛡 Drag a file/folder here to scan
No scans yet — drop a file above.
.\nDo you want to rescan the file?
and the file is no longer here:\n
A known malicious file is still on disk: {0}
No usable key and keyless mode is off — scanning unavailable.
No known live threats on disk.
🔴 {0} files were once clean but later turned into threats (see the History tab).
No scan in a while — keep current with 'Scan downloads' or 'Recheck'.
No usable API key — add a key from Settings or turn on keyless mode.
📤 {0} files in offline queue (will retry when internet returns).
Clear all scan history? (cache is not affected)
🔴 Became a threat later: {0} file(s) were once clean, now flagged (latest: {1} {2}). Click to review.
The file is no longer here:\n
This record has no file path.
No API key yet. Add one from the Settings tab.
{0}: click this notification to scan the drive.
{0} monitored files changed and lost their trust: {1}
🌙 Scheduled scan found {0} threats — click the notification to load them into the queue.
{0} threats found. Click to load them into the queue.
A monitored file is now more dangerous!
Delete {0} permanently?\nThis CANNOT be undone (the file is removed from the vault entirely).
Delete the {0} selected files permanently?\nThis CANNOT be undone.
Delete ALL {0} files in the vault permanently?\nThis CANNOT be undone.
Permanently delete quarantine records older than how many days?
Restore the {0} selected files to their original locations?
{0} files later turned malicious — {1} still on disk.
This file is no longer on disk — it cannot be quarantined.
❓ Help — what do the signals mean?

Ctrl+K command palette · Ctrl+F search · Ctrl+C copy SHA-256 · Ctrl+Shift+C verdict image (clipboard) · Ctrl+R rescan · Ctrl+Q quarantine · Ctrl+Shift+J select all threats · J/K jump to threat · Enter open VT report · Space pause/resume · F5 recheck verdict.
The result based on how many engines flagged the file. You can change the thresholds and names from Settings.
Are the detections from major/reputable engines, or only from minor engines? If only minor engines flagged it, it is most likely a false positive.
A signature match = the fingerprint of a known piece of malware (certain). Heuristic/ML = a guess (not certain). If all detections are heuristic, be cautious.
When the file was first seen in the world. A file first seen minutes ago is riskier than one known for years.
The malware family that engines commonly name (e.g. trojan, ransomware). It summarizes what kind of threat it is.
A valid code signature = the publisher's identity is verified. This is NOT a guarantee of being 'clean'; it only verifies identity. Signed files are not sent to VT to avoid spending quota.
Was the file downloaded from the internet, and from which site? Files coming from the internet should be examined more carefully.
What the file does: network communication, persistence, anti-analysis, keylogging, etc. (from VT tags, without running it).
Data appended to a signed file after the signature. It is normal in installers but can also be a sign of a stuffed/trojanized file.
If the detections come from signatures that are months old, it may make sense to recheck with up-to-date engines.
Makes a suspicious file unrunnable by changing its extension to .VIRUS. It is reversible — you can restore it from the Quarantine vault.
Uses VT's web interface via a hidden browser instead of an API key: keyless but slower.
⚠ Did it become a threat later?
Select a row that exists on disk.
Keyless (GUI) mode is off — this check needs keyless re-querying.
No file that later became a threat and is still on disk was found.
{0} files were once clean, now flagged and still on disk.
No folder repeatedly producing threats was found.
{0} folders repeatedly produced threats — consider closing the source instead of deleting one file at a time.
{0} persistence hooks found. Removal is reversible: the registry value is written to quarantine\autostart-restore.log, the Startup .lnk is moved to the vault.
No threat recurred across more than one scan.
{0} threats reappeared in separate scans — the source may still be live.
Scanning executables that arrived in the last {0} days (verdict from cache)…
Range: {0}  •  Scanned: {1}  •  Threats: {2}  •  Clean: {3}
Embeds itself to auto-start when Windows boots
Stays persistent by creating a scheduled task
Stays persistent by installing a system service
Hooks itself to an event trigger (runs at startup)
Injects itself into another process (to hide)
Obfuscates/encrypts its code to evade analysis
Detects virtual machine/sandbox and hides its behaviour
May encrypt files and demand ransom (ransomware)
Communicates with a remote server over the network
Downloads additional files/payload from the internet
Connects out using a non-standard protocol
Exfiltrates data over the command channel
Tries to extract passwords/credentials from the system
Runs when the user opens the file
Tries to spread to other machines on the network
Tries to look like a legitimate file (masquerading)
Writes an autostart/persistence key — survives reboot
Quarantine this file? (its extension is changed to .VIRUS so it can't be run)
Add 'Scan with VirusTotal' to the right-click menu? (administrator may be required)
Not found on VT or the lookup returned nothing (an API key is required to upload).
VirusTotal requested a reCAPTCHA. Please solve it below, then press the button on the right.
No space in the vault — free up at least {0} MB.
The vault file has changed since it was quarantined — restoring it is not safe. Use 'Permanently delete' instead.
A file already exists at the original location.
⚠ Claims to be '{0}' but the signature is {1} — could be IMPERSONATION
⚠ '{0}' is normally signed by '{1}'; this file is {2} — possibly a fake/trojanized build
No API key is defined. Add a VirusTotal key from Settings.
